guide.title: Get Started in 5 Minutes
guide.subtitle: From API key setup to voice input — a complete step-by-step guide

step1.title: Step 1: Get an API Key
step1.description: Sign up at groq.com (recommended — fast and has a free tier), openai.com, or openrouter.ai. Create an API key in the console. Desktop users can also use Ollama for local models.

step2.title: Step 2: Copy Your API Key
step2.description: Go to the API Keys page of your chosen provider, create a new key, name it (e.g. "VoxPen"), and copy it — it's only shown once.

step3.title: Step 3: Install VoxPen
step3.android: Search for "VoxPen" on Google Play, or download the latest APK from GitHub Releases.
step3.windows: Download the latest Windows installer (.exe) from GitHub Releases and run the setup wizard.
step3.linux: Download the AppImage from GitHub Releases. Run chmod +x VoxPen*.AppImage, then double-click or run from terminal.

step4.title: Step 4: Configure Your API Key
step4.description: Open VoxPen settings, find the "Speech" section. Select your provider (Groq, OpenAI, OpenRouter, or Ollama), paste your API key, and click "Test" to verify the connection.

step5.title: Step 5: Start Using
step5.android: Android: Go to Settings → Language & Input → Manage Keyboards → Enable "VoxPen Voice Keyboard". Switch to VoxPen in any app and tap the mic button.
step5.desktop: Desktop: Press the hotkey (default Ctrl+Shift+V) and start speaking. Release to auto-paste the transcription at your cursor.

cost.title: Cost

free.title: Free Tier
free.description: Groq's free tier provides 28,800 seconds (8 hours) of audio and ~500K text tokens per day. Normal daily usage (100 voice inputs × ~15 seconds each = 25 minutes) is well within the free limit — it costs nothing.

paid.title: Paid Tier Breakdown
paid.description: Even beyond the free tier, costs are minimal. Example: 100 voice inputs per day (~25 minutes total) + AI refinement:
paid.breakdown: Speech recognition (Whisper Turbo): 25 min ≈ $0.017/day | AI refinement (Llama 3.1 8B): ~40K tokens ≈ $0.005/day
paid.total: Total: under $0.03/day, about $0.60/month — less than a cup of coffee.

cost.note: Based on Groq pricing (Feb 2026): Whisper Turbo $0.04/hr, GPT-OSS-120B $0.15–0.60/M tokens. Other providers available — see pricing page for comparison.

q1.question: Forgot my API key?
q1.answer: Log in to Groq Console, delete the old key in the API Keys page, and create a new one. Paste it back into VoxPen settings.

q2.question: Inaccurate speech recognition?
q2.answer: Check your language setting ("Auto-detect" is recommended). Use in a quiet environment for best results. Enable "Smart Refinement" to let AI auto-correct recognition errors.

q3.question: What providers are supported?
q3.answer: Groq (recommended — fastest, free tier), OpenAI (GPT-5-nano is very cheap), OpenRouter (one key for Google, Anthropic, DeepSeek, etc.), and Ollama for running local models on desktop.

q4.question: Works offline?
q4.answer: VoxPen requires an internet connection for AI-powered speech recognition and refinement. Offline use is not currently supported.

privacyPage.title: Your Voice, Your Data
privacyPage.subtitle: How VoxPen's privacy-first architecture keeps your data safe

architecture.title: Privacy by Design
architecture.flow: Your Device → Direct to AI Provider → Text Response → Local Storage
architecture.noServer: No VoxPen server ever touches your data

byok.title: BYOK Model
byok.description: Bring Your Own Key — you get your own API key, and your voice data goes directly to your chosen AI provider (Groq, OpenAI, etc.). VoxPen never proxies, relays, or stores your data.

zeroCollection.title: Zero Collection Policy
zeroCollection.description: VoxPen requires no account registration, tracks no usage behavior, and sends no telemetry. No analytics tools, no crash reporters, no "anonymous usage statistics". What you use and what you say — only you know.

encryption.title: Local Encrypted Storage
encryption.description: API keys are protected by Android Keystore (Android) or Tauri encrypted store (Desktop) — never stored in plain text. Transcription history is in a local database that you can delete anytime.

concerns.title: Privacy Concerns with Voice Input Services
concerns.intro: Some subscription-based voice input services have these data flow risks:
risks: Your voice is first sent to the service provider's server, then relayed to the AI — an extra invisible intermediary
Account creation with payment info required — your identity and usage can be fully correlated
Usage behavior may be tracked for "service improvement" or "personalization"
Servers may temporarily cache or log your voice data, even when claiming otherwise

overview.title: Overview
overview.content: VoxPen (語墨) is an AI voice keyboard that operates on a BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) model. We do not collect, store, or transmit any user data to our servers.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection
dataCollection.intro: VoxPen does not collect any personal data.
items: No analytics or telemetry
No crash reporting to our servers
No user accounts or registration
No ads on any platform

audioData.title: Audio Data
audioData.intro: When you use VoxPen for voice input:
items: Audio is recorded locally on your device
Audio is sent directly from your device to the API provider you configured (e.g., Groq, OpenAI)
Audio is not stored on your device after transcription completes
VoxPen has no access to the audio data sent to your API provider
audioData.providerNote: Your API provider's privacy policy governs how they handle the audio data.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
items: API keys are stored locally on your device using Android EncryptedSharedPreferences (Android) or Tauri encrypted store (Desktop)
Keys are encrypted at rest using platform encryption mechanisms
Keys are never transmitted to any server other than the API provider you configured
Keys are never logged or displayed in full

transcriptionHistory.title: Transcription History
items: Transcription results are stored locally on your device in a local database
You can delete individual transcriptions or all data at any time
No transcription data leaves your device unless you explicitly share or export it

llmRefinement.title: LLM Refinement
items: Transcribed text is sent directly to your configured LLM provider (e.g., Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic)
VoxPen does not see or store the refined text beyond your local device
Your LLM provider's privacy policy applies to this data

permissions.title: Permissions
items: RECORD_AUDIO — Voice recording for speech-to-text
INTERNET — Sending audio to your API provider

dataDeletion.title: Data Deletion
dataDeletion.intro: VoxPen does not store any personal data on any server. All data is stored locally on your device. You can completely delete all data by following these steps:
items: Transcription history: Open VoxPen → Settings → Transcription History → Tap "Delete All" to clear all locally stored transcriptions
API keys: Open VoxPen → Settings → Speech → Clear the API Key field and save
Full removal (Android): Go to System Settings → Apps → VoxPen → Storage → Clear Data, or simply uninstall the app
Full removal (Desktop): Uninstall VoxPen — the application data folder will be removed along with it

dataTypes.title: Data Types: Deleted vs Retained
dataTypes.retained: VoxPen does not retain any data on any server, so there is no need to request remote data deletion from us. After uninstalling or clearing app data, all data is permanently deleted with no additional retention period.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.content: VoxPen communicates only with the API providers you explicitly configure. We do not integrate any third-party SDKs for analytics or tracking.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.content: VoxPen does not knowingly collect data from children under 13. Since VoxPen collects no data at all, this is inherently satisfied.

changes.title: Changes
changes.content: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Changes will be posted in the app's repository.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For privacy questions, please open an issue on our GitHub repository.

android.steps: Install from Play Store, or download APK and allow unknown sources.

windows.steps: Run the installer and follow the wizard. VoxPen appears in the system tray.

linux.steps: Run chmod +x VoxPen.AppImage, then double-click or run from terminal.

lightweight.title: Lightweight. Seriously.
lightweight.subtitle: Pro tools don't need to be another browser

meta.title: Why VoxPen? — Features, Performance & Privacy Compared
meta.description: Feature-level comparison vs Typeless, Tauri vs Electron performance, and privacy architecture analysis.

hero.title: Why VoxPen?
hero.subtitle: Features, privacy, performance, price — a full comparison

tech.title: Desktop: Lightweight Architecture
tech.subtitle: Same cross-platform goal, 30x difference in size

electron.title: How Electron works
items: Bundles an entire Chromium browser (~100MB)
Plus Node.js runtime (~30MB)
Every Electron app is a full Chrome instance

tauri.title: How Tauri works
items: Uses the system's built-in WebView (0MB extra)
Core is a compiled Rust binary (~5MB)
Native performance, minimal resource usage

quotes.title: What users are saying

items.text: Wispr Flow idles at 800MB RAM, drags down my VS Code
8-10 second startup, fans spinning like crazy
Why does every app need a built-in Chrome?

quotes.conclusion: This is why we chose Tauri.

concerns.title: Privacy Concerns with Voice Services

contextAwareTone.description: Detects your active app and auto-adjusts tone — formal for email, casual for chat. 22 built-in app rules plus custom overrides.

speakToEdit.description: Select text in any app, then voice-instruct AI to rewrite, translate, or fix it.

meta.title: Pricing — VoxPen vs Subscription Voice Input
meta.description: VoxPen + Groq costs less in a year than one month of subscription services. Real numbers, do the math.

hero.title: One price. Yours forever.
hero.subtitle: No subscriptions. No recurring fees. Pay once, use forever.

tiers.title: Simple pricing
tiers.subtitle: Free forever for light users. One-time purchase for unlimited. Mobile and Desktop sold separately.

calculator.title: What does API usage cost?
calculator.subtitle: Your BYOK key, your costs — here's what to expect (prices as of February 2026)
calculator.byokNotice: {{provider}} models are BYOK — costs start from first use. Switch to Groq for free tier.

comparison.title: Real Cost Comparison
comparison.subtitle: Based on a typical user with 50 voice inputs per day

scenarios.title: Cost by Usage
scenarios.footnote: Costs shown assume Groq models with free tier. OpenAI/OpenRouter costs vary by model.

reasons.title: No middleman
reasons.description: Subscription services charge you monthly, then call the same AI API with your money. VoxPen lets you call it directly — cutting out the middleman's profit.

reasons.title: No server costs
reasons.description: VoxPen runs no servers, no account systems, no data storage. Those savings go straight to your wallet.

reasons.title: Transparent pricing, no surprises
reasons.description: One-time purchase, BYOK API costs you control. No hidden fees, no auto-renewals, no price increases. You own it forever.

cta.title: Done the math?
cta.subtitle: One year of savings buys you AirPods. Two years? That's 10 fancy dinners out.

pricing.disclaimer: API costs based on Groq's February 2026 rates. Exchange rate: 1 USD ≈ 32 TWD. Subscription service prices sourced from their public pricing pages (annual plans). App purchases are one-time via Lemon Squeezy.
